origin.name: Bangladesh
origin.key_document: Death certificate in Bengali (requires official English translation)
origin.source: FCDO Bangladesh travel advice, updated 2025; British High Commission Dhaka guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation is available for non-Muslim deaths. Most British nationals of Bangladeshi origin are Muslim; full body repatriation is the usual practice. Bringing ashes home is possible where cremation is carried out.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Death certificate in Bengali (certified English translation required)
special_rules: For families of Bangladeshi origin in the UK, the Deputy High Commission in Sylhet is particularly important and handles many repatriation cases.
The death certificate is issued in Bengali and requires a certified English translation for UK acceptance.
Police and magistrate involvement is required for all unnatural or suspicious deaths.
An export permit from the relevant Bangladesh authority is required for all repatriations.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Bangladeshi documentation must be in certified English translation. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British High Commission in Dhaka and Deputy High Commissions in Sylhet and Chittagong can register the death and advise. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Bangladesh to UK Repatriation: Guidance for Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Bangladesh. Repatriation to the UK takes 2-3 weeks. Sylhet DHC, Bengali translation, and export permits all handled. Contact us 24/7.
